mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate revenue expectations rise.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with PAYEMS growth (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window, or the positive return hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, further increases signal severe aggregate demand contraction and rising credit risk, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:unemployment rate at 10.0% as of December 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a low federal funds rate and accelerating retail sales creates a liquidity-fueled demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days, as the interaction amplifies discount rate compression with revenue growth.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and RSAFS does not add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) or the 60-day forward return Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, positive real GDP growth surprises signal strengthening aggregate demand and corporate profit recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or insignificantly correlated with GDPC1 growth in post-recession quarters (Rank IC < 0.03), or the positive hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals manufacturing recovery and operating leverage gains, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings momentum surprises to the upside.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 45% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600 thousand units signal a structurally impaired residential construction sector and weak household formation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as cyclical demand expectations collapse.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts at 574K as of November 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST is below 600 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of declining unemployment and a federal funds rate below 1% signals a recovery that is supported by accommodative monetary policy rather than choked by tightening, producing strongly positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of UNRATE and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) or the 20-day forward return Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25%, the zero-interest-rate policy signals persistent deflation risk and compressed net interest margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of weeks when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.
